pregnancy.definition: the time when a woman has a baby growing inside her body
pregnancy.example: During pregnancy, she thought a lot about her future relationship with her child.

foundation.definition: the base that everything else is built on
foundation.example: A good relationship is built on a strong foundation of trust.

teenager.definition: a young person between 13 and 19 years old
teenager.example: Even if your child is a teenager, this chapter may help.

stuck.definition: unable to move forward, not changing
stuck.example: If your relationship feels stuck, these ideas may help.

repair.definition: to fix something that is broken or damaged
repair.example: It is possible to repair a relationship that feels stuck.

steer.definition: to guide or direct something in a particular direction
steer.example: This chapter may help steer you toward a loving relationship.

efficient.definition: doing things quickly without wasting time or energy
efficient.example: Parents sometimes treat children as things to be efficient about.

dominant.definition: most common, strongest, or most powerful
dominant.example: It is a dominant old-fashioned idea that children just need to be managed.

ideology.definition: a set of beliefs or ideas about how things should be
ideology.example: The old ideology says you can easily fit parenting into a busy life.

slot.definition: to fit something into a space or schedule
slot.example: Some parents try to slot parenting into their busy schedule.

forthcoming.definition: willing to share and talk openly
forthcoming.example: If you never listen, your child may not be very forthcoming with you.

reflect.definition: to think carefully and deeply about something
reflect.example: Pregnancy is a good time to reflect on your own childhood.

confined.definition: limited or kept within narrow boundaries
confined.example: She wanted a relationship not confined by roleplay.

roleplay.definition: acting a part instead of being your real self
roleplay.example: She wanted honesty, not a relationship confined by roleplay.

filial.definition: relating to the connection between a child and parent
filial.example: A bond can be more than just a filial tie.

charming.definition: pleasant and attractive in a way that makes people like you
charming.example: Her mother seemed like a charming, perfectly nice woman.

bouts.definition: periods of time when something bad happens
bouts.example: She had been having bouts of depression since the breakup.

depression.definition: a mental condition where you feel very sad for a long time
depression.example: She told her mother about her bouts of depression.

unacceptable.example: She realized her difficult feelings were unacceptable to her mother.

denied.definition: refused to accept as true or real
denied.example: Her mother's own difficult feelings were denied by her.

threat.definition: something that causes fear or danger
threat.example: When I feel low, maybe for her it feels like some sort of threat.

firmly.definition: in a strong way that will not change
firmly.example: That emotional door is firmly shut.

polite.definition: showing good manners, being respectful on the surface
polite.example: Their relationship was stuck at the polite-conversation stage.

duty.definition: something you feel you should do because it is expected
duty.example: She did not want her daughter to visit only out of duty.

resolution.definition: a firm decision to do something
resolution.example: She made a resolution to always be honest with her child.

fake.definition: not real, pretending to be something you are not
fake.example: She decided not to be fake with her daughter.

forceful.example: A baby gives you a forceful sincerity that you cannot ignore.

sincerity.definition: being honest and truly meaning what you say or feel
sincerity.example: She decided to pay her daughter the compliment of sincerity.

damnedest.definition: trying as hard as you possibly can (informal)
damnedest.example: She did her damnedest to be sincere with her child.

compliment.definition: here it means showing respect or honor to someone
compliment.example: Being honest with your child is paying them a compliment.

smiley.example: She accepted all of her daughter's moods, not just the smiley ones.

useless.definition: not able to do anything helpful, having no value
useless.example: When the baby cried, she sometimes felt useless.

frantically.definition: in a very worried, hurried way
frantically.example: She tried not to frantically fix whatever was wrong.

alongside.example: Instead of fixing the problem, she tried to be alongside her child.

manual.definition: a book of instructions that tells you how to do something
manual.example: She did not want to be a cut-out parent from a parenting manual.

expectant.definition: waiting for a baby to be born
expectant.example: Expectant parents should take the long view.

chores.definition: regular tasks that need to be done, usually not fun
chores.example: Children are not chores to feed and clean.

lifelong.definition: lasting for the whole of someone's life
lifelong.example: You are building a lifelong relationship with your child.

secure.example: A loving bond helps the child feel secure.

independent.definition: able to do things by yourself without help from others
independent.example: When your child becomes independent, the bond can still grow.

significant.example: Your child will have their own significant others and social network.

concerns.definition: things that worry you or that you care about
concerns.example: You can keep up with each other's lives and concerns.

insight.definition: a clear understanding of something, often something hidden
insight.example: On a later visit, she got some more insight into the problem.

split.definition: separated or broke apart (about a relationship ending)
split.example: She had depression since her partner and she had split up.

arm's length.definition: keeping a safe distance, not getting too close
arm's length.example: Their relationship was stuck at arm's length.
